L2.description: A memory corruption vulnerability exists in the data decompression bindings, where a deprecated C API is used that does not perform bounds checking on the output buffer. Crafted compressed input can cause an out-of-bounds write, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution.

L3.area: LZ4 compression library Go bindings and decompression routines
L3.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the LZ4 decompression bindings where a deprecated C API function is called without proper bounds validation on the output buffer. When processing crafted LZ4-compressed data, the decompression routine can write beyond allocated memory bounds, potentially allowing arbitrary code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The Uncompress function uses the deprecated LZ4_uncompress C API which does not perform bounds checking, allowing crafted compressed input to write beyond the allocated output buffer and corrupt memory.
